guardrails: Result copy must describe readiness, not approval.
Do not claim funding decisions or lender-specific outcomes.
Every variant should point to an educational next step or human review when appropriate.

variants.headline: Strong funding readiness
variants.body: Based on your answers, your file may be ready for a faster human review and funding-path conversation.

variants.headline: Your file looks review-ready
variants.body: Your answers suggest a stronger readiness profile. The next step is documentation review, not guessing.

variants.headline: Manual review recommended
variants.body: Your answers show potential, but a few details should be reviewed before choosing a funding path.

variants.headline: Close enough to inspect
variants.body: This looks like a file where human context matters. Review the documents and next steps before moving forward.

variants.headline: Selective-fit profile
variants.body: Some funding paths may be possible, but the file likely needs cleaner documentation or tighter expectations first.

variants.headline: Possible, but not automatic
variants.body: Your answers suggest a selective-fit profile. The smart move is to reduce blockers before requesting review.

variants.headline: Prep-first recommended
variants.body: Your file may need foundation work before a funding conversation makes sense. Start with the cleanup checklist.

variants.headline: Do not rush the application
variants.body: The answers point to readiness gaps. Fix the basics first so you do not waste shots with weak timing.
